name: Find Middle + Reverse + Merge
summary: Find the middle with slow/fast pointers, reverse the second half in place, then weave the two halves together — O(1) extra space.
visualizationIntent: init: show the list wired head-to-tail. phase 1 (find middle): slow advances one node, fast two, until fast reaches the end — slow lands on the middle. phase 2 (reverse): split after slow (that next link flips to null, flashing as a changed link), then walk the second half flipping each next pointer backward — every flip flashes. phase 3 (merge): first walks the front half and second walks the reversed half, and each step rewires first.next to second (a changed link flashes) then second.next to the saved next — weaving the two halves. return: traverse from the head and show the reordered value sequence.

lineExplanations.4: Start the next-pointer array (nextp[i] = the id node i points to).
lineExplanations.5: Loop over every node to wire up the original chain.
lineExplanations.6: Every node except the last points to the following node.
lineExplanations.7: Point node i at node i+1.
lineExplanations.9: The last node points at nothing (-1).
lineExplanations.10: changed marks which link just flipped (for the animation).
lineExplanations.11: slow starts at the head (the tortoise).
lineExplanations.12: fast starts at the head (the hare).
lineExplanations.13: While the list is long enough and fast can take two hops, keep going.
lineExplanations.15: Advance fast two nodes — when it reaches the end, slow is at the middle.
lineExplanations.16: second is the first node of the second half (just after the middle).
lineExplanations.17: If there is a second half to work with…
lineExplanations.18: …cut the list after slow (the first half now ends there).
lineExplanations.20: prev builds the reversed second half; start it empty (-1).
lineExplanations.21: curr walks the second half from its first node.
lineExplanations.22: Reverse the second half node by node while curr is valid.
lineExplanations.23: Remember curr's next before we overwrite it.
lineExplanations.24: Flip curr's next to point backward at prev.
lineExplanations.26: prev moves forward to curr (the new reversed head).
lineExplanations.27: curr moves on to the saved next node.
lineExplanations.28: first starts the merge at the head of the front half.
lineExplanations.29: second starts at prev — the head of the reversed second half.
lineExplanations.30: Weave the two halves while the second half still has nodes.
lineExplanations.35: second's next becomes the saved first-half continuation.
lineExplanations.36: Advance first to its saved next.
lineExplanations.37: Advance second to its saved next.
lineExplanations.38: Start an empty list to read out the reordered values.
lineExplanations.39: Begin the read-out traversal at the head.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def reorderList(self, values): values are the node values in order.
syntaxExplanations.5: for i in range(n): loops over each node index.
syntaxExplanations.6: if i < n - 1: true for every node except the last.
syntaxExplanations.7: nextp.append(i + 1) links node i to node i+1.
syntaxExplanations.8: else: handles the last node (bare else — no step of its own).
syntaxExplanations.9: nextp.append(-1) marks the tail's next as none.
syntaxExplanations.10: changed = [] holds the id of the link that just changed.
syntaxExplanations.11: slow = 0 starts the slow pointer at the head.
syntaxExplanations.12: fast = 0 starts the fast pointer at the head.
syntaxExplanations.13: while n > 2 and nextp[fast] != -1 and nextp[nextp[fast]] != -1: fast needs two hops ahead.
syntaxExplanations.14: slow = nextp[slow] advances slow by one node.
syntaxExplanations.15: fast = nextp[nextp[fast]] advances fast by two nodes.
syntaxExplanations.16: second = nextp[slow] is the start of the second half.
syntaxExplanations.17: if second != -1: only split when a second half exists.
syntaxExplanations.18: nextp[slow] = -1 cuts the list into two halves.
syntaxExplanations.20: prev = -1 seeds the reversal with an empty tail.
syntaxExplanations.21: curr = second starts the reversal walk.
syntaxExplanations.22: while curr != -1: reverse until the second half is consumed.
syntaxExplanations.23: nxt_node = nextp[curr] saves the next node before overwriting.
syntaxExplanations.24: nextp[curr] = prev flips curr's link backward.
syntaxExplanations.26: prev = curr moves the reversed head forward.
syntaxExplanations.27: curr = nxt_node continues along the original second half.
syntaxExplanations.28: first = 0 starts the merge at the head.
syntaxExplanations.29: second = prev is the head of the reversed second half.
syntaxExplanations.30: while second != -1: weave until the second half runs out.
syntaxExplanations.31: temp1 = nextp[first] saves the front half's continuation.
syntaxExplanations.32: temp2 = nextp[second] saves the reversed half's continuation.
syntaxExplanations.33: nextp[first] = second splices second after first.
syntaxExplanations.35: nextp[second] = temp1 links second to the saved front continuation.
syntaxExplanations.38: order = [] will collect the reordered values.
syntaxExplanations.39: cur = 0 if n > 0 else -1 starts the read-out at the head.
syntaxExplanations.40: while cur != -1: traverse to the end.
syntaxExplanations.42: cur = nextp[cur] follows the rewired next pointer.
syntaxExplanations.43: return order hands back the reordered sequence.
